strategy.name: Smart Money Concepts (SMC) — Institutional Alignment
strategy.description: Trades only where institutional footprints converge: order blocks, fair value gaps, and liquidity sweeps on the 4H entry frame, filtered by daily bias and confirmed on the 1H. No setup fires without multi-timeframe alignment. Prioritises a high win rate over frequency — missing a trade is always cheaper than taking a bad one.
philosophy: Institutions accumulate and distribute at specific price zones, leaving footprints that repeat.
Retail stop clusters (equal highs, equal lows) are targets — price sweeps them before reversing.
The best entries come after a liquidity sweep INTO an order block or FVG, not before.
Structure tells you where you are. Liquidity tells you where price is going next.
If the daily bias and the 4H setup disagree, do not trade.

bias.role: Determines directional bias for the session. Only trade longs if daily is bullish. Only trade shorts if daily is bearish. No counter-trend trades.
bias.read_from: Daily swing structure, SMMA(21) slope, last BOS/CHoCH on daily

entry.role: Where setups form. Identify order blocks, FVGs, and liquidity levels here. Price must be reacting at a 4H zone for a trade to be valid.
entry.read_from: 4H order blocks, 4H FVGs, 4H liquidity pools, 4H BOS

confirmation.role: Entry trigger. Wait for a 1H CHoCH (change of character) or BOS inside the 4H zone before entering. This reduces entering too early into a zone that has not yet shown reversal intent.
confirmation.read_from: 1H CHoCH, 1H BOS, 1H displacement candle, 1H FVG retest

execution.role: Optional precision entry. After 1H confirmation fires, drop to 15m to find the tightest entry and tightest stop, improving the risk-to-reward ratio.

smma_21.role: Primary trend filter. Price above SMMA(21) on daily = bullish bias, only look for longs. Price below = bearish bias, only look for shorts. Slope matters — flat SMMA means range, avoid directional bias.
smma_21.calculation: SMMA(n) = (SMMA_prev * (n-1) + close) / n, seeded with SMA(n) for first value

smma_50.role: Secondary confluence. Price trading above 4H SMMA(50) adds bullish confluence for longs; below adds bearish confluence for shorts. Not a standalone signal — use as confluence only.

volume.role: Confirms institutional participation. Impulse legs (displacement candles) must have above-average volume. Low-volume sweeps are weak and unreliable. Retracements into order blocks should come on declining volume.
volume.measurement: Compare current candle volume to 20-period average volume on the same timeframe

thresholds.retracement: Volume < 0.8x 20-period average — weak pullback, zone likely to hold
thresholds.sweep: Volume spike at sweep candle confirms stop hunt; low volume sweep = unreliable

atr_14.role: Stop placement and position sizing. Stops are placed at 0.5x ATR beyond the invalidation level, not at arbitrary percentages. Also used to judge whether a zone is within reasonable reach (within 2x ATR) or too far away.

order_block.definition: The last opposite-colour candle before a significant displacement move. Represents the zone where institutions placed large orders. Price frequently returns to this zone to rebalance.

bullish_ob.identification: Last bearish (red) candle immediately before a strong bullish impulse that breaks structure. The body of this candle is the order block zone.
valid_conditions: The move away from the OB must break at least one significant swing high (BOS)
OB has not been previously traded through and closed through on a higher timeframe
Price is returning to OB from above (retest), not approaching from below
bullish_ob.entry_zone: Between the low and close of the bearish OB candle (refined entry: 50% of the OB body)
bullish_ob.invalidation: A 4H close below the OB low

bearish_ob.identification: Last bullish (green) candle immediately before a strong bearish impulse that breaks structure.
valid_conditions: The move away from the OB must break at least one significant swing low (BOS)
OB has not been previously traded through and closed through
Price is returning to OB from below (retest), not approaching from above
bearish_ob.entry_zone: Between the high and close of the bullish OB candle (refined entry: 50% of the OB body)
bearish_ob.invalidation: A 4H close above the OB high

order_block.confluence_boost: An OB that also coincides with a FVG or a liquidity sweep is a premium setup — highest priority.

fair_value_gap.definition: A 3-candle imbalance where the market moved so fast that a gap exists between candle 1's high/low and candle 3's low/high. Represents inefficiency that price tends to return to fill.

bullish_fvg.identification: High of candle 1 < Low of candle 3. The gap between these two levels is the FVG.
bullish_fvg.entry: Price retraces into the FVG (between high of candle 1 and low of candle 3). Enter at the midpoint or lower 25% of the FVG.
bullish_fvg.invalidation: A candle closes below the FVG low (full fill with close below = invalidated)

bearish_fvg.identification: Low of candle 1 > High of candle 3. The gap between these two levels is the FVG.
bearish_fvg.entry: Price retraces into the FVG (between low of candle 1 and high of candle 3). Enter at the midpoint or upper 25% of the FVG.
bearish_fvg.invalidation: A candle closes above the FVG high

fair_value_gap.priority: FVGs embedded within order blocks (OB+FVG confluence) are the highest-quality setups. A standalone FVG without OB confluence requires additional confirmation.

liquidity_sweep.definition: A deliberate move by institutions to grab the stop orders clustered above swing highs (buy-side liquidity) or below swing lows (sell-side liquidity) before reversing. Retail traders place stops at obvious highs and lows — institutions hunt them.

buy_side_sweep.identification: Price wicks above or briefly closes above a previous swing high or equal highs, then rapidly reverses with a strong bearish candle
buy_side_sweep.significance: Signals that sell-side institutions are now in control after grabbing buy stops. High probability of downside continuation.
valid_conditions: The sweep candle must reject the level — a long upper wick is ideal
The sweep should be into or beyond the buy-side liquidity by at least a few ticks
A bearish displacement candle must follow the sweep on the same or next candle
Volume spike on the sweep candle confirms institutional activity

sell_side_sweep.identification: Price wicks below or briefly closes below a previous swing low or equal lows, then rapidly reverses with a strong bullish candle
sell_side_sweep.significance: Signals that buy-side institutions are now in control after grabbing sell stops. High probability of upside continuation.
valid_conditions: The sweep candle must reject the level — a long lower wick is ideal
A bullish displacement candle must follow the sweep
Volume spike on the sweep candle preferred

liquidity_sweep.highest_priority: A liquidity sweep that lands precisely in an order block or FVG is the single highest-probability setup in this strategy. This combination (sweep + OB or FVG) is the primary entry trigger.

break_of_structure.definition: A confirmed close beyond a significant swing high (bullish BOS) or swing low (bearish BOS), confirming that the market structure has shifted in that direction.

bullish_bos.identification: 4H candle closes above the most recent significant swing high
bullish_bos.significance: Confirms bullish market structure. Look for long setups on the subsequent pullback.
bullish_bos.rule: Only count BOS from a swing that was formed by at least 2 candles on each side

bearish_bos.identification: 4H candle closes below the most recent significant swing low
bearish_bos.significance: Confirms bearish market structure. Look for short setups on the subsequent pullback.

break_of_structure.note: BOS defines the bias on the entry timeframe (4H). It must align with the daily bias. A 4H bullish BOS against a daily bearish bias is a warning — do not trade it.

change_of_character.definition: The first BOS in the opposite direction of the prevailing trend. Signals a potential reversal, not just a pullback. Lower weight than a trend-aligned BOS.
change_of_character.bullish_choch: First higher high printed in a downtrend — possible reversal beginning. Use on 1H to time entries within a 4H zone.
change_of_character.bearish_choch: First lower low printed in an uptrend — possible reversal beginning.
change_of_character.usage: On the 1H confirmation frame: wait for a 1H CHoCH inside a valid 4H OB/FVG before entering. This is the confirmation trigger that price is reacting at the zone.

equal_highs_lows.definition: Two or more swing highs/lows at approximately the same price level. These are visible liquidity magnets — price will likely sweep them before reversing.
equal_highs_lows.usage: Mark equal highs as buy-side liquidity targets (likely to be swept in a bearish context). Mark equal lows as sell-side liquidity targets (likely to be swept in a bullish context). Never place a take-profit at equal highs/lows — price will run through them.
equal_highs_lows.trap_avoidance: Do not buy breakouts above equal highs or sell breakdowns below equal lows. These are the stops being hunted.

bullish: Price is trading above daily SMMA(21)
Daily chart shows higher highs and higher lows (bullish BOS confirmed)
Last significant daily move was a bullish displacement (strong up candle with volume)
Daily FVG or OB below current price (unmitigated — acts as support floor)
bearish: Price is trading below daily SMMA(21)
Daily chart shows lower highs and lower lows (bearish BOS confirmed)
Last significant daily move was a bearish displacement
Daily FVG or OB above current price (unmitigated — acts as resistance ceiling)
neutral_avoid: Daily SMMA(21) is flat (within 0.3% slope over 5 days)
Price is chopping around the daily SMMA(21)
No clear BOS in either direction on the daily
Daily is printing inside bars or doji clusters — no conviction

long.narrative: Daily bullish bias confirmed. Price has pulled back to a 4H bullish order block or FVG. Ideally a liquidity sweep below a swing low just swept sell-side stops. 1H CHoCH to the upside fires inside the zone. Enter.
required_all: Daily bias is BULLISH — daily SMMA(21) sloping up, price above it, higher highs and higher lows on daily
4H structure is bullish (last 4H BOS was to the upside)
Price has retraced into a valid 4H bullish order block OR bullish FVG — zone has not been previously closed through
1H CHoCH to the upside has printed inside or immediately above the 4H zone — confirms price is reacting
Entry candle (1H or 15m) shows rejection from the zone: lower wick, engulfing, or pin bar
strong_confluence_at_least_2: A sell-side liquidity sweep occurred at or just before the 4H OB/FVG (swept equal lows or swing low)
The 4H zone is also a daily OB or daily FVG (higher-timeframe zone confluence)
4H SMMA(50) is above price and acting as support at the same level as the OB/FVG
The displacement candle that created the 4H OB had volume > 1.5x the 20-period average
The retracement into the zone came on declining volume (weak sellers, no conviction)
An unmitigated daily FVG sits immediately below the entry zone (layered support)
long.minimum_score: All required conditions must pass. At least 2 confluence factors must be present. Score 4 or above out of 7 total confluence points.
long.minimum_rr: 2.5:1 — if the target (next liquidity or OB) does not offer at least 2.5R, skip the trade

short.narrative: Daily bearish bias confirmed. Price has retraced up to a 4H bearish order block or FVG. Ideally a liquidity sweep above a swing high just grabbed buy stops. 1H CHoCH to the downside fires inside the zone. Enter.
required_all: Daily bias is BEARISH — daily SMMA(21) sloping down, price below it, lower highs and lower lows on daily
4H structure is bearish (last 4H BOS was to the downside)
Price has retraced into a valid 4H bearish order block OR bearish FVG
1H CHoCH to the downside has printed inside or immediately below the 4H zone
Entry candle shows rejection from the zone: upper wick, bearish engulfing, or shooting star
strong_confluence_at_least_2: A buy-side liquidity sweep occurred at or just before the 4H OB/FVG (swept equal highs or swing high)
The 4H zone is also a daily OB or daily FVG
4H SMMA(50) is below price and acting as resistance at the same level
The displacement candle that created the 4H OB had volume > 1.5x average
The retracement into the zone came on declining volume
An unmitigated daily FVG sits immediately above the entry zone (layered resistance)
short.minimum_score: All required conditions must pass. At least 2 confluence factors must be present.

take_profit: TP1 (50% position): Next opposing liquidity pool — nearest equal highs (for longs) or equal lows (for shorts). Move stop to breakeven when TP1 is hit.
TP2 (remaining 50%): Next significant HTF order block or FVG in the direction of the trade, OR the daily swing high/low that would constitute the next BOS.
Do NOT set TP at round numbers — institutions hunt them. Add 5–10 ticks beyond the obvious level.
If price sweeps TP2 and prints a strong rejection candle, close early rather than holding for more.
stop_loss: Place stop 0.5x ATR(14) beyond the invalidation level of the zone — below the OB low for longs, above the OB high for shorts.
Never place stop at the exact swing high/low — it will be swept. Add buffer.
A 4H candle close through the entry zone invalidates the setup — close immediately, do not wait for the stop.
trailing: Once TP1 is hit, trail the stop below each successive 1H higher low (for longs) or above each 1H lower high (for shorts).
If a new 4H FVG forms in the direction of the trade, move stop to protect below/above that FVG.
early_exit_signals: A displacement candle in the opposite direction with volume > 1.5x average — close immediately.
Price prints a 1H CHoCH against the trade direction before reaching TP1.
A higher-timeframe OB or FVG in the opposite direction that was not accounted for at entry.

risk_rules: Risk exactly 1% of portfolio per trade — no exceptions. Position size is derived from stop distance, not fixed lot size.
Maximum 2 open positions simultaneously. If 2 trades are open, do not open a third until one closes.
Maximum 2 trades per day. More setups appearing means the market is choppy — not that more opportunity exists.
No trading in the 30 minutes before or after a major scheduled news event (CPI, FOMC, NFP). The spread widens and OBs get violated on the spike.
If the last 3 trades were losses, stop trading for the rest of the day. Do not revenge-trade.
Paper trade every new asset for a minimum of 20 trades before going live. The zones are asset-specific.
Do not trade if the daily candle is an inside bar or has a range smaller than 0.8x the 14-day ATR — no institutional conviction.
Minimum RR before entry is 2.5:1. If the math does not work, skip the trade.

mandatory_avoid: Counter-trend trades — if daily is bearish, do not take 4H long setups regardless of how clean they look
Trading into an unmitigated higher-timeframe OB or FVG in the opposite direction — price will likely reach it
Entering a zone that has already been tested twice — third test of a zone is a break, not a bounce
Any setup where the stop must be placed more than 2x ATR(14) from entry — risk/reward becomes unworkable
Trading during low-liquidity windows: weekends on crypto, first 15 minutes of session open, last 15 minutes before close
Breakout trades above equal highs or below equal lows — these are liquidity grabs, not momentum entries
preferred_sessions: London open (08:00–12:00 UTC) — highest institutional participation, cleanest OB reactions
New York open (13:00–17:00 UTC) — second-highest liquidity, frequent sweeps and reversals
London/NY overlap (13:00–16:00 UTC) — premium window for high-volume setups
avoid_sessions: Asian session (00:00–07:00 UTC) for BTC — lower volume, false signals, OBs frequently violated
Sunday open — gaps and erratic price action

confluence_scoring.description: Each setup is scored before entry. Minimum score of 4 required. Do not take a trade below 4.

conditions.condition: Daily bias aligns with trade direction
OB and FVG overlap at entry zone
Liquidity sweep into the entry zone
Daily OB or FVG at same level (HTF confluence)
4H SMMA(50) provides additional S/R at zone

sweep_and_reverse.description: Price sweeps a liquidity pool (equal highs or lows), lands in an OB or FVG, then 1H CHoCH fires. The clean version of this strategy.

ob_retest_after_bos.description: 4H BOS occurs. Price pulls back to retest the OB that caused the BOS. 1H CHoCH fires at the OB. Enter.

fvg_fill_with_rejection.description: Strong displacement creates an FVG. Price retraces to partially fill the FVG but rejects before full fill (rejecting at the 50% level). Volume declines on the fill.

htf_ob_reaction.description: Price reaches a daily OB or daily FVG that perfectly aligns with a 4H OB. HTF confluence is maximum. This is the highest-conviction setup.

notes: This strategy trades infrequently by design. 2–4 high-quality setups per week is the target — not per day.
If you find yourself looking for setups, you are forcing trades. Setups find you when structure is clear.
The daily bias check is the most important filter. Everything downstream of a wrong daily bias is noise.
A missed trade is not a loss. An impulsive trade is.
Journal every trade: score, what aligned, what didn't, what you ignored. Review weekly.
SMC concepts work because institutions create these footprints consistently — human psychology at the institutional level is predictable.
